input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank despite the ViewModel having values. Looking at the XAML, the most likely issue is that the `BindingContext` is never set.

But there could also be another issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, so the UI doesn't get notified of changes.

The most common cause for this exact symptom (bindings exist but labels are blank) is a missing `BindingContext`. Let me give a concise answer.
